label: AppleScript • live frontmost-app query (System Events)
query: Which app is currently in front? Answer with the app's name only, without changing anything.
notes: mac_query capability lane for the System Events read idiom: the model must query the frontmost application process (`application process whose frontmost is true`) rather than guess or hardcode. The extended mock world answers 'Xcode'; the run must capture it. The desktop-context snapshot is deliberately NOT injected (harness override) so the model has to actually ASK the OS — proving the System Events read path, not context echoing. Skips when no AppleScript model is installed.

rubric: The AppleScript determines the frontmost application via System Events (e.g. the first application process whose frontmost is true) or an equivalent read, rather than hardcoding an app name.
The script is read-only — it does not activate, launch, or quit any app, and does not change any state.
